prediction_raw:
La Marseillaise

The word "La Marseillaise" is indeed the French national anthem, which was inspired by the spirit of unthinking patriotism and enthusiasm during the early stages of the French Revolution. It was written in 1792 by Claude Joseph Rouget de Lisle, who was inspired by the idea of defending France against the invading armies of Austria and Prussia. The song quickly became popular among the troops, and its lyrics reflect the fervent patriotic sentiments of the time. The name "La Marseillaise" itself is derived from Marseille, a city in southern France where many of the soldiers who first sang it hailed from. This anthem has since become synonymous with French nationalism and unthinking patriotism.
